RYDER MEMORIAL HOSPITAL INC

CCN: 405018 · HUMACAO, PR 00792 · Humacao County

Overview

Address
355 AVE FONT MARTELO, HUMACAO, PR 00792
Phone
7877167977
Certified beds
62
Avg daily residents
23 (37% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare
Medicare/Medicaid since
1979-04-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Data not available.
— Not Available

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 1.94 3.90 1.92 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.51 0.68 1.50 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.42 0.87 0.42 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 0.00 2.35 0.00 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.94 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.13 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
1.63
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
1.25
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
1.62
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
73.1%
annual
RN turnover
72.2%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.38
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.00
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-26 13 13 0 124 1 124
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-15 17 17 0 208 1 208
Total weighted health score
145.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (41)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

41 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2025-03-26 2025-05-15
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2024-05-15 2024-05-24
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-05-15 2024-06-07
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-05-15 2024-05-24
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-05-15 2024-05-29
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-16
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-29
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-27
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. E 2024-05-15 2024-05-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-17
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2024-05-15 2024-06-03
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-05-15 2024-05-24
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2024-05-15 2024-06-15
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2024-05-15 2024-06-07
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2024-05-15 2024-06-17
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-20
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2024-05-15 2024-05-20
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-04-05 2023-05-30
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-04-05 2023-04-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-04-05 2023-04-10
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. F 2023-04-05 2023-04-11
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-04-05 2023-05-15
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. F 2023-04-05 2023-04-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-04-05 2023-04-25
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2023-04-05 2023-05-30
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2023-04-05 2023-05-15
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. E 2023-04-05 2023-04-09
